title: Ensure Cluster Check triggered at least once per week for  Kubernetes Clusters

input_row.title: Ensure Cluster Check triggered at least once per week for  Kubernetes Clusters
input_row.description: Kubernetes Engine's cluster check feature helps you verify the system nodes and components healthy status. When you trigger the checking, the process would check on the health state of each node in your cluster and also the cluster configuration as kubelet\docker daemon\kernel and network iptables configuration, if there are fails consecutive health checks, the diagnose would report to admin for further repair.
input_row.rationale: Kubernetes Engine uses the node's health status to determine if a node needs to be repaired. A node reporting a Ready status is considered healthy. The cluster administrator could choose to trigger the cluster check periodically. An cluster healthy checking including: • The cloud resource healthy status, including the VPC/VSwitch SLB and every ECS node status in cluster. • The kubelet, docker daemon, kernel, iptables configurations on every node in cluster. Kubernetes Engine generates the diagnose report when checking finish. You can check the diagnose suggestion on ACK console.
input_row.audit: Through the management console: 1. Logon to ACK console 2. Select the target cluster and open the More pop-menu for advance options on cluster. 3. Select Overview page on left column and check if the Last check status is Normal. 4. Verify the checking time and details in Global Check.
input_row.remediation: Through the management console: 1. Logon to ACK console 171 | P a g e 2. Select the target cluster and open the More pop-menu for advance options on cluster 3. Select Global Check and click the Start button to trigger the checking
input_row.default_value: By default, the cluster checking process is not auto triggered, the cluster administrator could start it in ACK console.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The audit approach is determined to be automated because the control can be verified programmatically using Alibaba Cloud APIs, Aliyun CLI, or Alibaba Cloud CLI. Although the initial audit description mentions a manual process through the ACK console, the rationale implies periodic checks can be automated. There are API methods available that can be used to verify the cluster's health check history and ascertain how frequently the checks have been triggered.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ali_ack_cluster_check_triggered_weekly

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **API Calls/CLI Commands**:
    - Use Aliyun CLI or Alibaba Cloud API to call the appropriate methods for checking the health of the Kubernetes cluster and track the last health check timestamp.
    - The specific API method to call could resemble `DescribeCluster` in the Alibaba Cloud Kubernetes API to retrieve cluster status and check the last health check.

2. **Resources to Enumerate**:
    - Identify all Kubernetes clusters in the relevant Alibaba Cloud regions.

3. **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate**:
    - Query the `LastCheckTime` or similar property to determine the frequency of the cluster checks.
    - Validate that the `LastCheckStatus` is `Normal`.

4. **Pass/Fail Criteria**:
    - **Pass**: The cluster check has been triggered at least once in the last week (7 days).
    - **Fail**: The cluster check has either not been triggered during that period, or the `LastCheckStatus` is not `Normal`.

5. **Example**: 
    - Use Aliyun CLI command `aliyun ack DescribeCluster --ClusterId <your-cluster-id>` to check for the `LastCheckTime` and determine if a check has been performed within the last week.
    - Validate that `LastCheckStatus` is `Normal` to ensure the cluster is healthy and that checks are being performed as required.
